(Said to a logician who argued that the ox might stand still and shake his bells so that the oilman could not infer from the sound of the bells that the ox was working the mill in his absence). mantiq baghārrā, yā chhāṅṭnā , v. n. (from نطق) s. f. Oration, logick. ¶ munt̤iḳ (part. act. IV.) adj. Rational (a number) in opposition to a surd.
subs: or mant̤iq ćhāṅṭnā, v.n. To chop logic.

s.m. That part of each Veda which comprizes the hymns (as distinguished from the Brāhmaṇas, or theological portions); — a passage of the Vedas, a holy text; — a formula sacred to any particular deity; — a mystical verse, a magical formula; an incantation, a charm, spell, philter; — secret consultation, concerting together; — spiritual instruction (i.q. gur-mantar); counsel, advice
more: n. m. S. मित्र A friend ( dost , 1).

n. m. S. मन्त्र 1. A spell; charm ( jādū , 1). Bichchhū kā mantar na jāne, sāṅp ke muṅh meṅ uṅglī ḍāle . Prov. The charm for scorpion's bite even he does not know, his hand into the snake's mouth yet must go. 2. ( mantr shāstar ) A mystical or magical formula; the prayers or incantations of the Tantras, a division of the Vedās. 3. ( gur mantr ) Spiritual instruction. 4. Counsel; advice. mantar phūṅknā, paṛhnā, yā mārnā, mantar paṛh-ke phūknā, mantar chalānā , v. a. To charm; cast a spell; to read a spell or incantation ( afsūn k .). mantar d ., v. a. 1. To give spiritual instruction. 2 To make a disciple.
s. m. 1. A charm, philter, spell, incantation. 2. Secret consultation, private advice. منترجنتر mantar-jantar ( मंत्रयंत्र ) Incantation, exorcism.
subs: (-par), or mantar paṛh-ke phūknā, or mantar paṛhnā (-par), or mantar-ćalānā, or mantar mārnā, To breathe, or to read or repeat, or to cast, a spell or incantation (over)

def: s.f. The act of consulting, consultation, deliberation; — advising, counselling; counsel; persuasion.

more: n. m. Counsellor; minister of state. 1. Rājā ho-ke mantrī mān, Lachhmī ho-ke suno Purān . Sikshā. A king you are, your counsellor hear; a rich man, to Purāns give ear! 2. Siyār ke mantrī kavvā, chhoṛ dihale hāṛ, chām, khāë lihale masuā . Bhoj. Prov. The jackal's minister the crow, the bones and skin he leaves, the meat he eats up, oh! param yā pradhān mantrī ; A. vazīr-i-āzam , n. m. The prime minister; premier. ku-mantrī , n. m. An evil counsellor.
s. m. An adviser, counsellor, kingʼs minister: enchanter.

s.m. = S منترتا मन्त्रिता mantritā, s.f. The condition, or office, or functions, of a minister (mantrī), or councillor; ministership, ministry; councillorship.
s.m. See منو manu.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian من (man), ultimately from Akkadian 𒈠𒈾 (manûm).

def: s.m. A man (in general); the representative man, and father of the human race, Manu, the legislator and saint, son of Brahmā, or personification of Brahmā himself (the name is, however, a generic term; and in every kalpa, or interval from one creation to another, there are fourteen successive Manus presiding over the universe, each of them for a period called manvantara; the first in order of these Manus is called Svayambhuva, as sprung from Svayam-bhū, 'the Self-existent,' and to him is ascribed the celebrated law-book known as the 'code of Manu'; the next five Manus in regular succession are called Svāroćisha, Auttami, Tāmasa, Raivata, Ćākshusha; the seventh Manu, called Vaivasvata or 'Sun-born,' is the Manu of the present period); — a symbolical expression for the number fourteen
subs: vulg. manuj, adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Manu-born, sprung from Manu; — a human being, a man
(˚jād = ja+ad), adj. & s.m. (f. -ā or -ī), Man-eating, devouring human beings; — a Rākshas
s.f. The code of laws promulgated by Manu
s.m. The period or age of a Manu (supposed to be equal to 12,000 years of the gods, or 4,320,000 human years).

ety: n. of place fr. نزل 'to alight, to descend,' &c.
def: s.f. A place for alighting, a place for the accommodation of travellers, a caravansary, an inn, a hotel; a house, lodging, dwelling, mansion, habitation, station; a mansion of the moon; story or floor (of a house); deck (of a ship); — a day's journey; — a stage (in travelling, or in the divine life); — place of destination, goal; boundary, end, limit
more: n. f. نزل descended. 1. A stage ( paṛāv , 1). Hasrat pe us musāfir-i-be-kas kī roiye Jo hak gayā ho pausihch ke manzil ke sāmne! Sarūr. Weep for the helpless traveller who lays him down and sighs, Footsore and weary on the road, the goal before his eyes. 2. A day's journey. 3. ( Ast .) A mansion of the moon ( nichhattar ). 4. A story or floor of a house. 5. A number (of boats or carts). manzil ba-manzil jānā ; H. paṛāo paṛāo jānā , v. n. To travel by stages. manzil pahuṅchānā , v. a. 1. To prove; bring home to one. 2. To perform one's obsequies. manzil denā , Mah.; bisrām d . Hin. v. a. To lay down a corpse for a while on the way to its resting place. manzil k., yā kāṭnā , v. a. To go one stage. manzil mārnā , v. n. To finish a difficult task. manzil-i-maqsūd , n. f. A goal; destination. manzil-i-maqsūd ko pahunchnā , v. n. 1. To reach one's destination. 2. To gain one's object. manzil h…

(noun of place of نزل Heb. and Aram. ) s. f. 1. A dayʼs journey, a stage. منزل کاٿنا manzil kāṭnā (dakh.) To finish a journey. 2. An inn, dwelling, station, mansion, story or floor of a house, deck, a mansion of the moon. ¶ munzil, part. act. (IV.) 1. Causing to alight, receiving hospitably. 2. Emittens semen. ¶ munzal, part. pass. Sent down, caused to alight or to descend.
subs: adv. From stage to stage; by stages; — at every atage
v.t. To convey (one) to his destination; to convey (one) to his last resting-place, to perform the obsequies of; — to bring (a thing) home to (a person), to prove to

or manzil karnā, To finish a journey; to get over a stage; to go one stage
v.n. To finish a difficult task
s.f. The goal of desire; goal, destination
To reach (one's) destination; — to gain (one's) object
A stiff journey or stage; a long stage or march.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مَنزِل (manzil), from Arabic مَنْزِل (manzil, “house, dwelling”). First attested in c. 1421 as Old Hindi مَنْزِل (mnzl /⁠manzil⁠/, “abode”).

To confirm; affirm. manzūr-i-nazar , n. m. 1. A favorite; mistress. 2. A protege; minion. peshgāh se manzūr kīyā gayā , G. G. Received the assent of.

def: part. Having beaten, &c. (see mārnā; — mār is frequently prefixed adverbially to other verbs, and intensifies or otherwise affects their signification, as will be seen from the following examples)
subs: v.t. To beat and drive away; to put to flight
v.t. To lay dead, to slay, kill; — to withhold (wages, &c.) wrongfully
v.n. To live by plunder or spoliation, to earn by robbery, to prey (upon)
v.t. To knock down; to complete the destruction of
(-ko), To take or obtain by plunder or robbery, to plunder, rob
v.n. 'To smite oneself and die,' to commit suicide; — to slay (some of the enemy) and die, to do and die (also mārke marnā)
v.t. To beat and drive back, to beat back; to overthrow.

ety: Pehl. mār; Zend māra, rt. mar; S. मार, rt. मर्
def: s.m. A snake, serpent
subs: s.f. Serpentine twisting and winding; circumvolution; crooked manœuvring, crafty device, intrigue, chicanery

ety: conj. part. of marnā; and = Prk. मरिअ = S. मृत्वा, rt. मृ
def: part. Having died, &c. (frequent as first member of verbal compounds, as mar-paćnā, &c., q.v.s.v. marnā).

ety: fr. mār-nā, q.v.
def: s.f. Beating, striking; — a beating, a blow, stroke; punishment; affliction; — battle; a firing (at), cannonading, bombarding; — a rushing upon, a crowding round; — plunder, extortion, exaction; — cure, remedy (e.g. khūjlī-kī mār ghī hai); — lure, temptation, snare; — a kind of stiff clay or loamy soil with sand and vegetable mould; a rich black soil
more: n. f. 1. Beating; striking. 1. Lohe kī manḍī meṅ mār hī mār . Prov. In the iron in art it's hammer hammer all. 2. Mār ke āge bhūt nāchtā hai . Prov. Before the whip devils dance. 2. A blow; stroke. 3. Wrath; famine. 1. Roṭī kī mār . 2. Tujh pe Bhagvān kī mār paṛe! A curse. 4. ( In Comp .) Killing; destroyer. 5. Lure; temptation; snare. 6. Cure; remedy. Khujlī kī mār ghī hai . 7. Stiff clay or loamy soil with some sand and vegetable mould; ( Bundelkhand ) a rich black loam. mār baiṭhnā , To strike; beat. mār bhagānā , v. a. To put to flight. mār paṛnā , v. n. To be beaten. Akhto ne pakāī bariyāṅ, Bakhto ne pakāī dāl, Akhto kī jal gaīṅ baṛiyāṅ, to Bakhto pe paṛī mār . The co-wife's song in a Puppet-show. Akhto cooked pulse balls, and Bakhto cooked some dāl , Burnt were Akhto's pulse balls, blows on Bakhto fall. mār-pīṭ, mār-piṭāī, mār-dhāṛ, mār-kuṭāī , n. f. Beating and bruising; fighting;…

s. f. (dakh. s. m.) 1. Beating. 2. Battle. 3. A blow. 4. In compos. Killing, killer. مارپاڙو mār-pāṛū, adj. (dakh.) Deadly. مارپڙنا mār paṛnā, To be beaten. مارپيٿ mār-pīṭ, s. f. Beating and bruising, drubbing. ماردهار mār-dhār, s. f. Thumping and beating severely. مار دينا mār denā, To smite, to beat. مار ڐالنا mār ḍālnā, To kill, to smite. مار سکنا mār saknā, To be able to beat. مار کٿاٴي mār-kuṭāʼī, s. f. Beating and bruising. مار کهاني mār-khānī, To get a drubbing. مار گرانا mār girānā, To knock down. مار لانا mār lānā, To rob, to take by robbery. مار لينا mār lenā, To smite, to overcome, to conquer. مار مرنا mār marnā, 1. To commit suicide. 2. To fall in battle after killing some of the enemy. مارهارنا mār hārnā, To beat severely. مار هٿانا mār haṭānā, To overcome, to beat and drive back. مار मार mār, s. m. A name of Kāmadeva. See کام kām.

s. m. A snake, a serpent. مارپيچ mār-pech, s. f. Circumvolution, serpentine twisting and winding. مارپيچ کي راه mār-pech kī rāh, A crooked or winding path. مار گير mār-gīr, s. m. A snake-catcher. مار گيري mār-gīr-ī, s. f. Snake-catching.
subs: (-par), A beating (or punishment) to fall (upon); to be beaten
mār-piṭāʼī, mār-dhār, or mār-kuṭāʼī, s.f. Beating and bruising; assault and battery; fight, affray, struggle
(-ko), To give a beating (to), to beat, to smite
adj. & s.m. Quarrelsome, turbulent; — a turbulent fellow, a brawler
v.n. To suffer a beating, to get a drubbing, to be beaten (by, -se)
(-ko), To get (another) a beating, to cause (one) to suffer punishment, to have (one) punished; to punish
To go in plunder or spoliation, &c., to be made away with.

ety: see mar-nā
def: s.m. Dying.

ety: mār, q.v. + Prk. अओ = S. अ+कः
def: s.m. A striking, beating; a blow, &c. ( = mār); an attack (as on a fort, &c.), bombarding, &c.; a line (of fire); range or reach (of fire); — a violent and general destruction; — overwhelming profusion or plenty (of anything)
more: adj. 1. Struck down; smitten. 1. Kāl kā mārā . Famine-struck. 2. Sardī kā mārā . Frost-bitten. 2. Killed; slain. mārā jānā , v. n. 1. To be killed, slain. Chāpke se mil mujh se, dugānā, tere vārī jāūṅ! Muft meṅ aisā na ho maiṅ kahīṅ mārī jāūṅ! Wom. 2. To be ruined, wrecked, undone. 3. To be overturned or sunk (a boat). 4. To be cut off (a caravan, detachment, etc.). 5. To be lost. 6. To go away; to be non est. Sāre naukar kahāṅ māre gaë? Are all the servants dead? mārā mār, mār , n. f. 1. Struggle; bustle. 2. Jostle; rush; crush. Kām kī mārā mār hai . 3. Scuffle; fray; broil. mārā mārā phirnā, mārā phirnā , v. n. 1. To wander about. 1. Ṭuk hirs o havā ko chhoṛ, miyāṅ! mat des ba-des phire mārā! Nazīr. 2. Aisī hotī kātan-hārī to kāhe ko phirtī mārī . Prov. Could you have spun so you would not have been knocked about so. 2. To be knocked about. mārā mār k. , v. a. 1. To work hard; try one'…

Rus. adj. See bodā , (1, 3). 1. Weak. Māṛā mānas . 2. A little. Arī kisāṅṛīṅ, māṛā sā sīt manne bhī de! Rus. O country woman, give me too a little buttermilk! 3. Poor. Māṛī sākh . 4. Incompetent. 5. Bad. Māṛā ghī nāṅ hai . 6. Trifling. Māṛīchīj . 7. Mean; low. Māṛī bāt nā kaheṇ saëṅ . 8. Close-fisted. Māṛā jī hai .

adj. 1. Beaten, smitten, slain. 2. Foundered, or overturned (a boat, &c.). 3. (subst.) A victim. مارا پڙنا mārā-paṛnā, To be killed or slain. مارا ماري mārā-mārī ( मारामारि ) 1. Mutual beating, scuffle, fray, bickering, broil. 2. Preposteræ veneris usus reciprocus. مارا مارا پهرنا mārā mārā phirnā, To wander.
subs: s.f. Beating and buffeting, &c. ( = mār-kuṭāʼī, q.v.s.v. mār)

or mārā-mārī, s.f. Mutual beating or striking; contention, struggle, scuffle, broil, fray; — jostling; bustling; rush, crush; varied and toilsome efforts (as after the means of subsistence, &c.); — exceeding plentifulness (of); — preposterœ venis usus reciprocus: mārā-mār karnā, To bustle vehemently about; to make all haste; to toil hard; to try one's best

ety: perf. part. of mārnā, and = Prk. मारिअओ = S. मारित+कः
def: part. Beaten, smitten; struck down; killed, slain; destroyed, ruined, spoilt; foundered or overturned (as a boat, &c.); cut off; lost, &c. (see mārnā); — s.m. One who is beaten or stricken, &c. (by); a victim
subs: v.n. To lie (or be) killed or slain; to lie ruined or destroyed (as a field of corn, &c.)

v. pass. To be killed or slain; — to be overcome; — to be destroyed, or ruined, &c.; — to be overturned or sunk (as a boat, &c.); — to be cut off (as a caravan, or a detachment, &c.); to be lost, to be not forthcoming
or mārā phirnā, v.n. To wander about; — to be knocked about

ety: perf. part. of marnā, q.v.; — and = Prk. मलिदओ, or मलिअओ; S. मर्(मृ)+इत+कः
def: part. (f. -ī), Dead; dried up, withered; half-dead-and-alive; poor, wretched, miserable, &c.; — s.m. A corpse; carcass; a half-dead-and-alive person, a miserable creature; — base metal
subs: v.n. To move or go along at a half-dead-and-alive pace, to creep along
lit. 'A dead louse'; a miserably distressed, or wretchedly poor person; — a very slow person.

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Killing, slaying, destroying; — killer, destroyer (as last member of compounds); — death; pestilence, plague, epidemic disease; — the god of love (Kāmdev); the passion of love; — slaying, killing; — hindrance, impediment, obstruction
subs: (˚ra+āt˚), adj. & s.m. Essentially destructive, murderous, fatal, deadly; of a cruel disposition; — a cruel person, &c.

subs: adj. & s.m. (f. māṛī-sī), Little; — a little, a trifle.

def: s.m. The time or place for any necessary business.

def: s.f. Authority, rule, dominion.

wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مرد (mard).

def: s. (dakh.) Fight, conflict, scuffle.

s.m. pl. (of mirfaq, q.v.), Things by which one profits, or gains advantage or benefit; — appurtenances or conveniences of a house (as the kitchen, sink, water-closet, and the like); — elbows, or elbow-joints.
s.m. pl. (of marḥala, q.v.), Stations, stages, days' journeys; journeys.
more: s. plur. (of مرحله from رحل) Journies, stages, daysʼ journies.

def: v. a. رحم had mercy on. To bestow; grant.

n. f. S. महाराष्ट्री belonging to Mahraiṭā country. 1. A class of songs ( lāunī , 4). 2. Maladmininistration under the Marhaṭās. Marahṭī nahīṅ hai ki chāho jise lūṭ lo, yeh Aṅgrezī hai! marhaiṭī k. , v. a. To plunder; ravage. marhaiṭi ghis ghis . Anarchy; misrule.
(part. pass. of رأي Heb. ) adj. Visible. ¶ marī (from مرأ) s. The gullet.

ety: mar˚, prob. = mal(nā
def: part. Twisted; wound up; bound round.
more: adj. Twisted, wound up, bound round.

def: v. n. 1. To be slain. 2. To be overcome. 3. To be overturned or sunk (as, a boat). 4. To be cut off (a caravan, detachment, &c.).
adj. & s.m. (f. -ikā), Killing, slaying, putting to death; — killer, slayer, destroyer, executioner; murderer; — any deadly or pestilential disease, plague, pestilence; — a falcon, hawk.
s.m. An epidemic, a pestilential disease, contagious disorder; a plague; murrain; mortality.
more: n. f. 1. A twist. 2. Crookedness. 3. See اکرَ (6, 7, 8). 4. A fracture. 5. A formula or a special rule to solve a difficulty. 6. A trick; manœuvre. maṛak'nā , v. n. 1. See اکرَنا 3, 4, 5. 2. To crack; break. Chūṛiyāṅ maṛak gaīṅ .

def: s.m. = مرك marak, q.v. adj. & s.m. (f. -ikā), Killing, slaying, putting to death; — killer, slayer, destroyer, executioner; murderer; — any deadly or pestilential disease, plague, pestilence; — a falcon, hawk.

ety: for A. معرکة maʻrakat, n. of place fr. عرك 'to rub, press, scrape,' &c.

more: n. m. 1. Strife; fight. 2. ( mārka-i-kār-zār ) A battle field. mārke-kā , adj. Important; momentous. mārke kā ādmī , A great man.
(Heb. from عرک Heb. and Chald. ) s. m. A field of battle. معرکه گير maﻌraka-gīr, A ropedancer.
subs: adj. (f. -kī), 'Martial, warlike'; — of importance; important, momentous
A man of importance, a great man
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian معرکه (ma'raka), from Arabic مَعْرَكَة (maʕraka).

s.m. An emerald.
Morocco (a country in North Africa); Marrakech (the capital city of the Marrakesh-Safi region, Morocco)
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مَرَاکِش (marākiš), from Arabic مَرَّاكِش (marrākiš).

def: adj. Soft, mild, bland; tender; — s.m. A species of goose (with red legs and bill); a duck; a horse; a grove of pomegranate trees; a cloud; lampblack (used as a collyrium); — a villain, rogue, scoundrel.
more: 1. s. m. A duck. 2. adj. Soft, mild, bland.

def: s.f. Repairing, rectifying, mending; reparation, repair; rectification, amendment
more: n. f. رم mending. 1. Repairs. 2. Amendment. marammat-talab , adj. Broken; out of order or repair. marammat karānā , v. a. To get repaired. marammat k. , v. a. 1. To repair; mend. 2. ( Slang ) To chastise; beat. 3. To break. maram'matī , adj. 1. Repaired. 2. Requiring repairs; broken.
(inf. of رمّ Heb. and Chald. ) s. f. Mending, repair. مرمت کرنا marammat karnā, To mend, to repair.
subs: adj. Wanting repair, in need of repairs, out of order; broken
v.t. To get (a house, or a thing) repaired
v.t. To repair, mend; rectify, set or put right.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مرمت (marammat), Arabic مَرَمَّة (maramma). Compare Punjabi مُرَمَّت (murammat) and Nepali मर्मत (marmat).

ety: fr. marammat
def: adj. Repaired, mended; — in need of repair; out of order; broken.
s.m. Killing, slaying, slaughter.

ety: see marnā
def: s.m. Dying; death; dying with shame; — ruin, destruction
more: n. m. S. मृ to die. 1. Death. 2. Dying with shame. 3. Ruin, destruction. Apnā maran, jagat kī hāṅsī! Prov. Death to you, mirth for the world!
subs: s.f. Place of death, mortal spot, vital part (of the body)
or maran-kāl, s.m. Time of death, the dying moments.

more: Slang, v. a. To suffer sodomy.

def: adj. See s.v. marnā. v.t. To beat, strike, thump, smite; to hit, knock; to punish; — to kill, slay, slaughter; to destroy, ruin, mar, spoil, blight, blast; to crack, break; to deaden or blunt (the edge of a knife, &c.); to destroy the active qualities of, to kill (quicksilver, lead, &c.), to calcine; — to strike out; to thrust out; — to attack (villages, houses, or travellers); to rob or seize with assault (i.e. with killing, or personal violence), to plun der; to embezzle; to withhold wrongfully; — to earn smartly and cleverly; to make (profit in business); — to stop, close; to fine; — to overcome, conquer, vanquish, defeat; to seize, take, capture, catch; to master subdue. tame, mortify (affections, appetites, or lusts); to calm, quench, allay, suppress, smother; to counteract (poison, &c.); — to press down; to stamp; — to drive; to drive or ram in; to insert; to apply, fix, fasten, clap on; — to set; to throw, to pelt with, to cast (a spell); to toss; to shoot, discharge, fire (a gun), dart or hurl (a missile), lay on (a sword, &c.); to fight (a battle); to strike or make (a sudden assault); to strike up (a run, dance, strain, &c.); to strike out (the hands or legs); to pitch (a tent); to chuck in, or toss off (a morsel); to raise or set up (a cry); to make or do (generally with vehemence, eagerness, or smartness); — (as a gerund) s.m. Striking, beating, &c.
subs: v.t. (intens.) To kill outright, to slay, to murder
v.t. (intens.) To beat completely, to overcome, conquer, &c.

def: s. m. Smiter, slayer.
s.f.(?) The pit of an old well that has gone to ruins.

more: s. plur. (of مرسة) Ropes. ¶ maris, s. f. Mode, manner, habit, custom.

ety: Pehl. mard, mart; Zend mareta; S. मर्त्य
def: s.m. A man; a male; a husband; — a brave or valiant man, a hero
more: n. m. 1. A male; a man. Mard kī sūrat ho-kar kamāyā nahīṅ jātā . In form and face a man, but earn nothing you can. 2. A husband. Mard kā dikhāyā na khāīye, mard kā lāyā khāīye! Wom. Prov. Eat not before your husband, eat what he brings you! 3. A fellow; chap; wight. Mujh se bāteṅ na ab ziyādah banā! Chal, cha/?/khe mardue! havās meṅ ā! Mah. Wom. Shauq. 4. A brave man; a hero. mard-bāz , Slang. adj. Lascivious, lustful (woman). mard-raugan , n. m. Semen. mard kī zāt . The male sex. mard kī sūrat na dekhnā , v. n. lit . not to see a man's face. subs: s.m. pl. Brave deeds, heroic achievements.

(part. pass. of ردّ Heb. ) adj. Rejected, excluded, reprobated, repulsed, confuted, abandoned; (subst.) An apostate, a reprobate.

Oppression, injuring man, tyranny, robbery and murder. مردم چشم mardum-i-chashm, The pupil of the eye. مردم خيز mardum-ḵẖez, Producing good men. مردم داري mardum-dār-ī, s. f. In August who each morn rice-milk doth eat Will bound along as doth the antelope fleet. mirg-trishnā ; A. surāb , n. m. The mirage. mirg-chiṛā , n. m. 1. A small bird. 2. Mirg-nainī, haṅs-gamnī . 2. Mirg kī sī āṅkheṅ, chīte kī sī kamar .

def: s.m. A vital part (of the body), vulnerable or mortal spot; tender place; weak organ, or part, or point (in); — any vital member or organ; the core (of anything); the quick; a joint; — anything which requires to be kept concealed, secret foible; secret design or purpose; hidden meaning; — anything hidden or recondite; secret recess (of the heart, &c.), the innermost heart; — a secret, mystery; truth
more: n. m. S. मर्म्म 1. Hidden meaning or purpose; a secret. 1. Bindrāban ke birachh ko maram na jāne koë, Ḍāl ḍāl aur pāt pāt meṅ Har kī charchā hoë . The heart of Bindraban's bright groves none know, Each branch and leaf with Hari's praises glow. 2. Rāg tāl ke maram na jāne, dono hāth majīrā! Prov. Nor song nor time he knows, cymbals both hands enclose! 2. Mind; heart. 1. Mātā tere rāj māṅ sukhī rahī din rāt, Ib koī īsā nahīṅ jo sune maram kī bāt . Rus. Wom. Mother! happy I was with you night and day, Now no one to listen to my heart's sad lay. 2. Dharā ḍhakā sab pūchhan lāge kuṭum, putr, pari-vārā, Darad, maram kī koī na pūchhe—matlab kā jag sārā . Bhajan. 'Where is your money hid? his relatives all keen to know, Nor dying wish nor groans they heed—all selfishness below.
s. m. Secret meaning or purpose, a secret, any thing hidden or recondite.
subs: or marma-vedī, adj. & s.m. (f. -ā, and inī), Knowing weak or vulnerable points; knowing secrets or the hidden circumstances (of any affair); knowing mysteries, familiar with the most secret or recondite portions of a subject, deeply versed (in); any very acute or learned man; a Brāhmaṇ; a spy
or maram-sthān, s.m. A vital part, vulnerable place; a tender spot, &c.

def: v.t. To beat, strike, thump, smite; to hit, knock; to punish; — to kill, slay, slaughter; to destroy, ruin, mar, spoil, blight, blast; to crack, break; to deaden or blunt (the edge of a knife, &c.); to destroy the active qualities of, to kill (quicksilver, lead, &c.), to calcine; — to strike out; to thrust out; — to attack (villages, houses, or travellers); to rob or seize with assault (i.e. with killing, or personal violence), to plun der; to embezzle; to withhold wrongfully; — to earn smartly and cleverly; to make (profit in business); — to stop, close; to fine; — to overcome, conquer, vanquish, defeat; to seize, take, capture, catch; to master subdue. tame, mortify (affections, appetites, or lusts); to calm, quench, allay, suppress, smother; to counteract (poison, &c.); — to press down; to stamp; — to drive; to drive or ram in; to insert; to apply, fix, fasten, clap on; — to set; to throw, to pelt with, to cast (a spell); to toss; to shoot, discharge, fire (a gun), dart or hurl (a missile), lay on (a sword, &c.); to fight (a battle); to strike or make (a sudden assault); to strike up (a run, dance, strain, &c.); to strike out (the hands or legs); to pitch (a tent); to chuck in, or toss off (a morsel); to raise or set up (a cry); to make or do (generally with vehemence, eagerness, or smartness); — (as a gerund) s.m. Striking, beating, &c.
more: v. a. See pīṭnā . 1. To beat; strike; thrash; knock down. 1. Mujh ko na māre to maiṅ sāre jahān ko mār āūṅ . ( Ironic .) Prov. Let no one strike me and I'll whip all creation. 2. Qadar-dān kī jūtīyāṅ uṭhāīye, nā-qadre ke pāposh mārne na jāīye . Prov. Hold the shoes of the just, deign not to shoe-beat the unjust. 2. To punish. 3. To drive away. Kutte ko māro! 4. To spurn; despise. Jūtī par mārtā hūṅ . 5. To kill; slay. Mārūṅ sūar, na jabhūṅ gāë, Kalmāṅ paṛhūṅ, na Rām kahāë . Kabīr. Nor pig I slay, nor slaughter e'er a cow, Nor read Koran, nor to Ram's name I bow. 6. To destroy; ruin. 7. To slay with kindness. Khūb rokā shikāëtoṅ se mujhe, Tū ne mārā ināëtoṅ se mujhe . Zauq. Complaints that I could make you've hushed right well, Your favours seal my lips as with a spell. 8. To keep down; bear up. Bhūk yā piyās mārnā . 2. Aise jogī phireṅ bahut se bhabūt-dhārā, jī, Nahīṅ karī īndrī bas, āpā…

( मारण or caus. of مرنا marnā, q. v.) v. a. To smite, strike, hit, kill, slay, beat, bang, drive, shoot, punish, mortify, cast, mar, blight, blast, spoil, ruin, conquer, crack, destroy, break, fine, take, set, smother, stamp, sting, stop, calm, throw, toss, quench, run. (subst.) Striking, &c.
subs: v.t. (intens.) To kill outright, to slay, to murder
v.t. (intens.) To beat completely, to overcome, conquer, &c.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀫𑀸𑀭𑁂𑀇 (mārei), from Sanskrit मारयति (mārayati), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *māráyati, from Proto-Indo-European *moréyeti, from *mer- + *éyeti.

ety: mar˚ = Prk. मर(इ) = S. म्रिय(ते), but Vedic also मर(ति), rt. मृ
def: v.n. To die, expire; to cease; to be dead (as in a game); to fade, wither; to be lost (as a stake, or a bad debt); to become bankrupt or insolvent (in these senses mar-jānā is the more com. form); — to die or be dying (for, -par), to set the heart (upon), to be desperately enamoured (of), or in love (with), to desire vehemently; — to labour or toil hard (for, -ko), to sweat and groan, to suffer hardship; — (as a gerund) s.m. Dying, death
more: v. n. 1. To die; expire. 1. Dhī marī, jaṅvāī chor; ṭūṭ gaī ḍālī, uṛ gayā mor . Prov. The daughter dead the son-in-law skulks stealthily alone, when breaks the branch the bird is flown. 2. Ādmī mualā par, chīj bhulailā par sūjhelā . Bhoj. Prov. When men and things have passed away their worth then to your heart you lay. 3. Kauṛī, kauṛī māyā joṛī, joṛ jamīṅ meṅ dhartā hai, Jiske lahne kī hai voh khāgā, pāpī bah bah martā hai . Bhajan. Farthings and pence heaped up, a store of wealth in earth hid lies, For him t' enjoy whose fate it is, the miser pines and dies. 4. Ann binā jaise pirān dukhī taise chhoṭe balam kī nār dukhārī, Oṛhat hūṅ ilṛī dulṛī, mohe lāgat ang aṅgār se bhārī, Aur abhākan ko pahne mohe pāyan mhāvaṛ lāgat bhārī, Aise jīvan se mar jāno bhalo, barsāt gaī piyā sang na soī! Kabit. As bread without life pines so aches the bosom of lone wife, The jewels which I wear worse than ho…
v. n. 1. To die, to expire, to cease. 2. ( met. ) To desire vehemently, to set oneʼs heart upon any thing, to dote. s. m. Dying.
subs: v.n. To work (oneself) to death, to labour excessively; to suffer pain or sorrow
v.n. To die; to fade, &c. (see marnā); to die away, die off; to be as though dead; to swoon
v.n. To recover from death; — s.m. Escape from the jaws of death
v.n. To die; to be dead; to lie dead; — to be dying (for, -par), &c., see marnā
v.n. To die and be effaced; to be killed or slain; — to be ruined, be sacrificed (for, -ke pīćhe)
or marne-hārā, adj. (f. -ī), & s.m. Doomed to die, dying, perishable, mortal; — a mortal
adj. Fit or deserving to die
About to die, at the point of death
To have no time to die; to be overwhelmed with work, to have too much to do to think of dying
To die prematurely; to die a violent death.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀫𑀭𑀇 (maraï), from Sanskrit मर॑ति (márati).

ety: prob. fr. the preceding
def: s.f. Paste; starch (see māṛī, and māṅḍī).

ety: S. स्मर+मोहः
def: s.f. Love, tenderness, affection, kindness, mercy, pity, compassion, humanity.
more: s. f. Affection, kindness, humanity, pity.

ety: maroh, q.v.+ī = Prk. इओ = S. इकः (इन्+कः)
def: adj. & s.m. Affectionate, kind; merciful, compassionate; — humane; — an affectionate, or a compassionate, person.
s.f. (dialec.) = maroṛ, q.v.; — s.m. = maroli, q.v.
s.m. = next, q.v.

ety: S. मरोलिकः
def: s.m. The sea-monster makar, q.v.
s.f. = مروڙ maroṛ, q.v.
more: n. f. See اکرََ 4—8. Twist; writhe; contortion. maroṛ-bāz , n. m. See akaṛ-bāz. maṛoṛ-phalī , n. f. Helicteres isora , the fruit of which is used as a cure for gripes.

def: s.f. = مروڙ maroṛ, q.v.

ety: fr. maroṛnā, q.v.
def: s.f. Turn, bend, flexion, twist, convolution, writhe, contortion (syn. akaṛ, q.v.)
more: (from مروڙنا) s. f. Twist, flexion, turn, writhe, convolution, contortion. مروڙباز maroṛ-bāz, An affected person.
subs: s.m. An affected person; a fop; a swaggerer
s.m. The screw-tree, Helicteres isora, and its fruit (which is used medicinally for gripes).

def: s.m. = مروڙا maroṛā, q.v.
more: n. m. Strain; gripes; tenesmus. maroṛā uṭhnā , v. n. To have gripes, or colic.

ety: maroṛ+Prk. अओ = S. अ+कः
def: s.m. Twist; contortion ( = maroṛ); — twisting of the bowels, gripes, colic; flux; tenesmus: — māroṛā uṭhnā v.n. Twisting of the bowels to arise; gripes or colic to come on.
more: (from مروڙنا) s. m. A twisting of the bowels, pain in the bowels, flux, gripes.

def: s.m. = مروڙا maroṛā, q.v.
